displayTitle: Detection Is a Ratio, and Gain Can't Buy It

beats.narration: In eddy current, a flaw is detectable only when its response stands clearly above everything else the coil reports. Detection is a ratio.

payload.title: SIGNAL OVER NOISE
payload.sub: The number that decides if a flaw is findable.

beats.narration: Measure both peak-to-peak, same channel, same settings. Procedures set a floor, commonly three to one, on the smallest required flaw.
Here's the trap most never escape. Eddy current noise enters through the coil with the signal, so gain lifts both equally.
Most ET noise is real impedance response, so it carries phase. Rotate lift-off horizontal and a crack breaks upward, clear of the wobble.

payload.heading: Noise has direction: separate it by phase
payload.note: LIFT-OFF HORIZONTAL · CRACK BREAKS UPWARD · GATE THE VERTICAL

beats.narration: So when the ratio is marginal, never raise the threshold. Fix something real: frequency, probe, filtering, fixturing, surface. Change the physics, not the picture.

payload.sub: Gain enlarges the picture; setup changes the ratio.
